name: Do I need acclimation before hardwood or laminate installation?

mainEntity.name: Do hardwood or laminate floors need to acclimate before installation in the Bay Area?
mainEntity.text: Do hardwood or laminate floors need to acclimate before installation in the Bay Area?

acceptedAnswer.text: Acclimation is often required before installing hardwood, laminate, and some engineered flooring products. It gives the material time to adjust to the home’s temperature and humidity, which reduces expansion and contraction after installation. In coastal areas, where indoor moisture can change more often, acclimation becomes even more important. The exact acclimation time depends on the manufacturer, the flooring type, and the site conditions. Some products need only a short period, while others require several days. The flooring should usually be stored in the same room where it will be installed, with the HVAC system running under normal living conditions. If a contractor says acclimation is unnecessary, that may be fine for certain products but not for all. The key is to follow the manufacturer’s instructions exactly. Ask what the acclimation period is, how the material will be stored, and what environmental readings must be met before the install starts.
acceptedAnswer.description: Many hardwood and laminate products need acclimation before installation so the material can adjust to the home’s temperature and humidity. Skipping acclimation can increase the risk of gaps, buckling, or warranty problems, especially in humid coastal environments.
